# STANCE — Wheel & Tire Fitment Calculator

> A free, browser-based wheel and tire fitment calculator. Compare two wheel-and-tire setups and see poke, offset (ET), inset, rolling diameter, rolling radius, ride-height change and speedometer error update live, with a technical cross-section diagram. Runs entirely client-side — nothing entered leaves the browser. "Tire" (American English) and "tyre" (British English) refer to the same part.

## What it does
- Compares a current setup and a new setup side by side and reports the difference between them.
- Outputs: poke, inset, offset (ET), overall rolling diameter, rolling radius, circumference, ride-height change, and speedometer error.
- Supports metric (mm, km/h) and imperial (in, mph) units.
- Shows a fitment verdict (Mild / Moderate / Aggressive / Extreme) based on the change in poke.

## Formulas used (lengths in millimeters; 1 inch = 25.4 mm)
- Poke = (rim width in inches × 25.4 ÷ 2) − offset (ET)
- Inset (backspacing) = (rim width in inches × 25.4 ÷ 2) + offset (ET)
- Rolling diameter = (rim diameter in inches × 25.4) + 2 × (tire width × profile ÷ 100)
- Rolling radius = rolling diameter ÷ 2
- Circumference = rolling diameter × π
- Ride-height change = (new rolling diameter − old rolling diameter) ÷ 2
- Speedometer error % = (old circumference − new circumference) ÷ new circumference × 100
- True speed = indicated speed × old circumference ÷ new circumference

## Key terms
- Poke: how far the outer lip of the wheel and tire sits past the hub mounting face.
- Inset / backspacing: how far the inner lip sits inboard; the clearance needed before the wheel fouls the strut, spring or control arm.
- Offset (ET): distance between the wheel's mounting face and its centerline; a higher ET tucks the wheel in, a lower or negative ET pushes it out.
- Profile / aspect ratio: sidewall height as a percentage of tire section width.

## Accuracy note
Rolling diameter uses the standard formula (rim + twice the sidewall) and does not model tire stretch, so overall-diameter figures can differ by a few millimeters from a stretched fitment. Poke, inset, offset, ride-height and speedometer figures are exact.

## Important
This tool provides estimates. A real-world fit also depends on bolt pattern, center bore, suspension geometry, camber, fender clearance and the tire's load rating. Always verify those before buying wheels or tires.

## Pages
- Calculator and full guide: https://wheelfitmentcalculator.com/
